learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate an awareness of recent developments in research into effectively teaching in inclusive primary classroom environments and the differentiation of teaching in ways that are responsive to the learning strengths and needs of students across the full range of abilities and that take account of the impact on learning of diverse backgrounds (e.g. linguistic, Indigenous, cultural, religious and socio-economic).

Critically reflect on legislative requirements, system policies and processes, positive behaviour supports and teaching strategies that promote student wellbeing and safety, and support participation and learning of all students with a focus on those with disabilities.
Plan challenging learning goals and effectively organise classroom activities for all students using age-appropriate curriculum and evidence-based approaches to differentiated teaching.

Explain approaches for interpreting student data and strategies for working effectively, sensitively and confidentially with others, including parents/carers and draw on external professionals and community representatives’ expertise to action an individual education plan and modify teaching practices.
Demonstrate clear, fluent, and coherent communication skills consistent with personal, professional and academic conventions.

assessments.task_description: In the tutorial in weeks 2, 3, 9, 11, 12, and 13, there will be an in-person task which must be completed on the sheet provided. The tasks are related to in-class tutorial activities on the weekly topic, mainly in short answer form. They are pass/fail. Doing before-class prep and weekly reading and reviewing lecture content will be helpful. Students must complete and pass 3 out of 6 in-person tutorial tasks in order to pass this course overall.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students will be given the tutorial activities in their designated tutorial. They will need to complete the task in their tutorial and submit the task before they leave the tutorial. These tasks will be handwritten unless there is an accessibility requirement.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Late submission is not possible for this piece of assessment as it must be completed in the tutorial for secure assessment purposes.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.assessment_title: Assessment Task 2: Adjusted Lesson Plan Development and Justifications
assessments.task_description:
Please ensure you carefully read the task description and marking criteria on the course Blackboard.

In this task, you will plan a 40-minute lesson with a major learning activity with differentiations to meet the needs of a range of students such as those with disability, from diverse linguistic, cultural, religious, and socio-economic backgrounds, Indigenous learners, and with a range of abilities. This lesson plan will also provide adjustments for students with a learning support need, selecting one from the four options as specified by the Nationally Consistent Collection of Data categories (Physical, Sensory, Cognitive or Social/Emotional). 

The lesson plan and activity must be applicable to a year level of your choice based on the Australian Curriculum and within your area of specialisation (e.g., Mathematics/Numeracy; English/Literacy; Science; Humanities and Social Sciences).

Using the principles of Universal Design for Learning (UDL), you will plan a 40-minute lesson demonstrating teaching strategies that support student participation and learning as well as strategies for assessing what students know or are able to do as a result of the lesson. In addition to the general planning with differentiations for all, you will plan adjustments based on the Task 1 chosen learning support need, indicating appropriate adjustments for teaching and assessing students with that need.

Include:

  • A brief overview (about half A4 page) explaining the context for the lesson such as: teaching area, year level, topic, and the chosen learning support need you have addressed and a discussion of relevant APST standards. This is to provide background for the lesson.
  • A lesson plan, in the format of your choice (in a table template, a maximum of two and half A4 pages). First establish a learning intention or firm goal that provides achievable challenges for students of varying abilities and characteristics. This table format should include the lesson topic, sequences of the activity, resources, “differentiations for all” and “adjustments for the focused need”, with a clear indication of the UDL features applied (the number of corresponding considerations, e.g., 3.1). (All resources should include enabling and extending prompts[1].)
  • Explanations and justifications of adjustments designed to meet the chosen learning support need based on evidence from academic and/or research literature including course readings, and a brief explicit linkage to the legislative requirements. (maximum one A4 page)
  • References (This section is not counted in the four pages requirement)
  • Acknowledgement of use (or not) of AI.

[1] Enabling and extending prompts represent a technique developed to support learners across a range of abilities. See: Sullivan, P., Mousley, J., & Zevenbergen, R. (2006). Teacher actions to maximize mathematics learning opportunities in heterogeneous classrooms. International Journal of Science and Mathematics Education, 4, 117-143.

The assignment is formatted in a Word document or PDF (2cm margins, 11-point font, 1.5 line spacing). The table of the lesson plan can be formatted in minimum line spacing. 

Use of APA conventions (7th ed.)

A guide for APA referencing is available on the following UQ library website: https://guides.library.uq.edu.au/referencing/apa7

Use of Inclusive language

Strict adherence to the UQ Guide to Using Inclusive Language is required. See detail on https://staff.uq.edu.au/files/242/using-inclusive-language-guide.pdf

Use of AI

If you use AI and/or machine translation (MT) technologies in any way in your assessment task, you must provide specific details about how it was used. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute academic misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Guidelines about conventions for appropriate referencing and acknowledgement, including an example coversheet to provide details of use, are available here: Overview - AI tools for assignments - Library Guides at University of Queensland Library
assessments.submission_guidelines: The assignment is to be submitted to a Turnitin link on the eLearn site. Aim to submit at least a day prior to the due date so you can check the originality report and revise your referencing if necessary. The percentage value is not always indicative of incorrect citation. Check the similarities and re-edit or revise them where necessary. You are allowed to have unlimited attempts until the due date and time. Only the last submission will be marked. Allow yourself plenty of time to submit your final copy by the due date and time.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Assessment Task 3: Case Study Report: Response to Scenario
assessments.task_description:
Please ensure you carefully read the task description and marking criteria on the course Blackboard.

This is a culminating task where you will build on your understandings of building inclusive classrooms by documenting preparations for a meeting with parents.

Scenario:

The parents of a student in your year 2 class have asked to meet with you to discuss how you will accommodate their child’s learning needs as specified in the Personalised Learning Plan (PLP) (or Individual Education Plan, IEP)*. Please see the PLP/IEP on the course eLearn site.  

In this task, you will document your preparation for the meeting, including the following:

Background

  1. A brief discussion of the relevant legislation and policy documents and a teacher’s legal obligations, related to this scenario. (Three levels of legislation including choosing a jurisdiction where you plan to teach). Legislative and academic references should be included. 
  2. An indication of the assessment data and school/classroom records you would study prior to the meeting to provide background on the student’s progress. Acknowledge the sources where appropriate. 
  3. A plan of adjustments to be made based on learning, assessment, and behaviours as appropriate to the student’s needs. Justify your plan based on research evidence and subject matter expertise.
  4. An overview of your investigation of applicable external professionals and community representatives including professional support networks, such as disability associations, and other community groups for extending your personal professional knowledge and practice. (In a table including the title, URL, key features and utility)
  5. A brief summary of relevant research evidence identifying how to effectively engage with families as partnerships in promoting learning appropriate to the student’s stage of learning.


A plan for the meeting

6. A plan for the meeting including: 

  • Location, timing, seating arrangement, refreshments etc.
  • Introductions – opportunity for parents to indicate what they wish to gain from the meeting
  • How you will indicate to parents that you seek their input and advice and how you intend to collaborate with them strategically through the year
  • Explanation of learning adjustments applicable to subject areas (including your specialisation) in response to the student’s PLP/IEP
  • Explanation of reasonable adjustments planned for assessment, o  in response to the student’s PLP/IEP.
  • Explanation of your plan for the student’s wellbeing and safety, including positive behaviour support processes (Tier 1, 2 or 3, as applicable) and classroom environment considerations.  

7. Follow up

  • Keep a record of the conversation
  • Further meetings
  • Reporting to others (e.g., school learning support team) 


8.References and acknowledge of use or not use of AI (This section is not counted in the word count)  


* Individual Learning Plan (IEP) is a generic term used internationally. Different school systems may use other terms. Education Queensland uses the term of Personalised Learning Plan (PLP). Each year, a new PLP would be generated for students with additional educational needs using the current model required by Education Queensland.  

Use headings to guide the reader. In Sections 1-3, use formatting: 2 cm margins, 11-point font, 1.5 line spacing. Organise Sections 4-6 in table format and use formatting that suits the table format.  

Use of APA conventions (7th ed.)

A guide for APA referencing is available on the following UQ library website: https://guides.library.uq.edu.au/referencing/apa7

Use of inclusive language

Strict adherence to the UQ Guide to Using Inclusive Language is required. See detail on https://staff.uq.edu.au/files/242/using-inclusive-language-guide.pdf

Use of AI

If you use AI and/or machine translation (MT) technologies in any way in your assessment task, you must provide specific details about how it was used. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute academic misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Guidelines about conventions for appropriate referencing and acknowledgement, including an example coversheet to provide details of use, are available here: Overview - AI tools for assignments - Library Guides at University of Queensland Library
assessments.submission_guidelines: The assignment is to be submitted to Turnitin link on the eLearn site. Aim to submit at least a day prior to the due date so you can check the originality report and revise your referencing if necessary. The percentage value is not always indicative of incorrect citation. Check the similarities and re-edit or revise them where necessary. You are allowed to have unlimited attempts until the due date and time. Only the last submission will be marked. Allow yourself plenty of time to submit your final copy by the due date and time.
